title: Semiconductor stocks rise on Big Tech earnings, Qualcomm beat
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Semiconductor stocks rose due to Big Tech earnings and Qualcomm's strong performance, driven by AI chip demand. Qualcomm's stock surged on earnings beat and AI
excerpt: Semiconductor stocks rose due to Big Tech earnings and Qualcomm's strong performance, driven by AI chip demand. Qualcomm's stock surged on earnings beat and AI chip sale. Market optimism about AI and semiconductor demand boosted sector.

content.introduction: Semiconductor stocks rose due to Big Tech earnings and Qualcomm's strong performance, driven by AI chip demand. Qualcomm's stock surged on earnings beat and AI chip sale. Market optimism about AI and semiconductor demand boosted sector.
body: Semiconductor stocks rose due to Big Tech earnings and Qualcomm's strong performance, driven by AI infrastructure investments. Qualcomm's stock surged on positive earnings and future AI chip deals.

45.   News • 6 days ago After an earnings beat, Intel’s stock is blasting through a dot-com-era ceiling
    46.   Breaking News • 7 days ago Nasdaq and S&P 500 rise as Intel stock soars, Dow slips amid Hormuz tensions
    47.   News • 7 days ago Meta to buy Graviton chips from Amazon as CPU sales soar [...] The maker of smartphone processors said it sees the market for Chinese phones bottoming in the current quarter. Earlier this week Qualcomm shares surged after tech analyst Ming-Chi Kuo from TF International Securities said that OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) is working with the company to develop smartphone processors.

What else you need to know:Semiconductor stocks have been on fire in recent weeks amid investors’ growing focus on agentic AI, in which bots or agents act on users’ behalf.

_Ines Ferre is a senior business reporter for Yahoo Finance. Follow her on X at__@ines\_ferre__._

What happened: Semiconductor stocks rose on Thursday.Qualcomm (QCOM) surged 9% in early trading, while Broadcom (AVGO), and AMD (AMD) climbed higher as well. Memory and storage players Sandisk (SNDK), Western Digital (WDC), and Seagate (STX) also rose.

What’s behind the move: Big Tech earnings from companies like Meta Platforms (META) and Microsoft (MSFT) showed hyperscalers plan to grow their capital expenditures on artificial intelligence infrastructure. [...] Wall Street sees chip infrastructure players needed for AI data centers as beneficieraes of the AI trade. Meta and Microsoft on Wednesday called out rising costs for data center components such as memory chips. The cost of memory and data storage has risen, benefiting companies like Sandisk (SNDK), Western Digital (WDC), and Seagate (STX), which posted strong results .

Meanwhile, Qualcomm’s better-than-expected first quarter results also lifted the broader chip sector. The company said it expects to ship custom silicon to an unnamed hyperscaler later this year.

# Semiconductor stocks rise on Big Tech earnings, Qualcomm beat

Yahoo Finance

Ines Ferré

0

Semiconductor stocks rise on Big Tech earnings, Qualcomm beat

QCOM stock today: Qualcomm Inc (QCOM) is seeing a sharp rally, with shares jumping more than 8% in early trading. The move reflects renewed optimism and it’s also happening just days before a key earnings report that could decide whether this momentum holds.  
  

## QCOM Stock Today Jumps As Intel Earnings Boost Lifts Semiconductor Stocks [...] For Qualcomm, this is a positive signal. Strong investment in AI infrastructure often supports demand in areas like mobile, automotive, and edge computing, segments where Qualcomm is deeply involved. When enterprise and cloud spending rise, it usually reflects broader health in the chip sector. [...] A big part of the boost is coming from Intel Corp, which posted a much stronger-than-expected quarter. The company beat earnings and revenue estimates by a wide margin, signaling improving demand across the semiconductor industry, as per a Benzinga report.  
  
That matters for Qualcomm. When the broader chip ecosystem strengthens, it tends to lift sentiment across multiple companies, even those focused on different markets.
  
  
Intel also pointed to strong growth in artificial intelligence, with AI-related revenue now making up a large portion of its business.

Results for the most recent quarter were rosier by comparison. The semiconductor company reported revenue of $10.6 billion for the March quarter, down 3% from the previous year and in line with what analysts tracked by FactSet were expecting. Adjusted earnings were $2.65 a share, coming in slightly ahead of the consensus of $2.56.

Amon said in a statement that the rise of agentic artificial intelligence is “reshaping” the company’s product road map.

Leading into the report, investors had become enthusiastic about a report saying that Qualcomm was being tapped to work with OpenAI on chips for an agentic-AI smartphone.

See more:Qualcomm’s stock gains as an AI opportunity emerges
keyTakeaways: Key developments in Semiconductor stocks rise on Big affecting market outlook
Semiconductor stocks rose due to Big Tech earnings and Qualcomm's strong performance, driven by AI infrastructure investments.
Qualcomm's stock surged on positive earnings and future AI chip deals.
content.outlook:
Looking ahead, Positive Sentiment: CEO said Qualcomm will begin shipping custom data‑center chips to a “large hyperscaler” this calendar year, reviving hopes for a material new revenue stream and helping offset concern about near‑term weakness. Read More.
   Positive Sentiment: Quarterly results: non‑GAAP EPS of $. Market participants will continue to monitor developments closely for signals about the direction of company news trends and their broader implications for portfolio allocation and risk management strategies.
